APPLICATION PURPOSE: FRESNO COUNTY RURAL TRANSIT AGENCY (FCRTA) IS CONSTRUCTING A NEW OPERATIONS AND MAINTENANCE FACILITY IN THE CITY OF SELMA CALIFORNIA. FCRTAS NEW STATE-OF-THE-ART BUS MAINTENANCE AND OPERATIONS FACILITY WILL INCLUDE A 10000-SQUARE-FOOT MAINTENANCE SHOP TO SERVICE FCRTAS VEHICLE FLEET A 5000-SQUARE-FOOT OFFICE 2 SOLAR BUS PORTS CHARGING INFRASTRUCTURE AND A BUS WASH. FCRTA WAS AWARDED FUNDING FROM FTA THROUGH THE LOW OR NO EMISSION OR THE GRANTS FOR BUSES AND BUS FACILITIES COMPETITIVE PROGRAM FOR PHASE II OF THE SELMA MAINTENANCE FACILITY PROJECT. PHASE II IS COMPRISED OF A SOLAR FIELD A SOLAR BUS PORT AND ELECTRIC VEHICLE (EV) CHARGING INFRASTRUCTURE AND BATTERY BACK-UP STORAGE. FOR THIS PROJECT $2162886 WAS AWARDED TO THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA ON BEHALF OF FCRTA TO PURCHASE THE ELECTRIC CHARGING INFRASTRUCTURE INCLUDING SOLAR PANELS FROM THE 2023 LOW OR NO EMISSION GRANT PROGRAM UNDER THE GRANTS FOR BUSES AND BUS FACILITIES PROGRAM. THE AWARD ID IS D2023BUSC008.; ACTIVITIES PERFORMED: PURCHASE CONSTRUCTION AND INSTALLATION OF A SOLAR FIELD SOLAR BUS PORT EV CHARGING INFRASTRUCTURE AND BATTERY STORAGE IN PHASE 2. PLANNING AND ENVIRONMENTAL REQUIREMENTS HAVE ALREADY BEEN COMPLETED AS A PART OF PHASE 1.; EXPECTED OUTCOMES: THE PROJECT WILL IMPROVE SAFETY IMPROVE THE CONDITION OF THE TRANSIT SYSTEM IMPROVE THE RELIABILITY OF THE SERVICE AND ENHANCE ACCESS AND MOBILITY.; INTENDED BENEFICIARIES: RURAL FRESNO COUNTY RESIDENTS; SUBRECIPIENT ACTIVITIES: FCRTA IS THE SUBRECIPIENT AND WILL WORK WITH THE DESIGN BUILD CONTRACTOR ZUMWALT CONSTRUCTION INC. TO PROVIDE THE ADDITIONAL SCOPE OF WORK (PHASE 2) ON THIS PROJECT THAT IS CURRENTLY UNDERWAY.

DOWNEY LAKE IS ON A PIECE OF PROPERTY OWNED BY A PRIVATE LANDOWNER ADJACENT TO THE ZUMWALT PRAIRIE WHICH IS OWNED AND MANAGED BY THE NATURE CONSERVANCY (TNC), BUT THIS SPECIFIC PARCEL IS SURROUNDED BY A DIFFERENT PRIVATE LANDOWNER. THE LARGER PIECE OF THE PRIVATE PARCEL OF LAND IS APPROXIMATELY 3,200 ACRES OF NEARLY CONTIGUOUS LAND AND IS IN A PERPETUAL CONSERVATION EASEMENT AND DOWNY LAKE SITS ON A 100 ACRE PARCEL. THE PROPERTY PREDOMINANTLY CONSISTS OF NATIVE IDAHO FESCUE (FESTUCA IDAHOENSIS), BLUEBUNCH WHEATGRASS (PSEUDOROEGNERIA SPICATA), SANDBERG S BLUEGRASS (POA SECUNDA) AND OTHER PRAIRIE GRASSES, FORBS, AND WOODY SPECIES ALONG WITH THE WIDE VARIETY OF WILDLIFE THAT THIS UNIQUE PLATEAU AND CANYON GRASSLAND ECOSYSTEM SUPPORTS. FURTHERMORE, THE PROPERTY SUPPORTS SPALDING S CATCHFLY, A NATIVE PLANT THAT IS FEDERALLY LISTED AS THREATENED UNDER THE ENDANGERED SPECIES ACT. APPROXIMATELY 50 ACRES OF EMERGENT WETLAND HABITAT ASSOCIATED WITH DOWNEY LAKE, A MID-ELEVATION PLAYA LAKE, AS MAPPED BY THE NATIONAL WETLANDS INVENTORY, HAS SPALDINGS CATCHFLY IN THE SURROUNDING AREA. THE PROPERTY IS LOCATED ON AN ECOLOGICAL BOUNDARY BETWEEN THE ZUMWALT PRAIRIE, A 250,000-ACRE PLATEAU GRASSLAND LANDSCAPE, AND THE DEEP CANYON GRASSLANDS OF HELLS CANYON, HOME OF THE SNAKE RIVER. COLLECTIVELY, THESE TWO ECOLOGICAL SYSTEMS REPRESENT THE BEST REMAINING EXAMPLES OF PACIFIC NORTHWEST BUNCHGRASS ECOSYSTEMS AND INCLUDE SEVERAL CATCHFLY KEY CONSERVATION AREAS (KCA). THE RELATIVELY UNFRAGMENTED CONDITION OF THE LANDSCAPE MEANS THAT IT IS A PLACE WHERE ECOSYSTEM PROCESSES (INCLUDING POLLINATION, REPRODUCTION AND GROWTH, AND DISTURBANCE REGIMES OF HERBIVORY, DISEASE, AND FIRE) CAN OPERATE AT MEANINGFUL SCALES. THIS LAKE IS A UNIQUE LAKE SITTING AT ABOUT 5,000 FEET IN ELEVATION AND IS KEY FOR MIGRATORY BIRDS PASSING THROUGH THE REGION AS A STOPOVER, ELK AND OTHER HERBIVORES USE IT, AND OTHER FLORA AND FAUNA
